what: Five documented fixes relative to the paper’s reference code
title: Method details and design decisions
text: published algorithm implemented exactly; published reference code contained implementation artifacts package fixes. documented anyone comparing psAve output original scripts understands small discrepancies arise. Integer simplex grid. reference code built grid expand.grid() {0,0.05,…,1}M\{0, 0.05, \dots, 1\}^M kept rows rowSums(gr) == 1 — exact floating-point equality test. M=4M = 4 step 0.05, silently dropped 187 1,771 valid simplex points (10.6%), including potentially optimal mixtures. simplex_grid() enumerates integer compositions, every valid grid point present construction. Proper weighted-eCDF KS. reference Fks computed ks.test() covariate values multiplied weights within arm, paper’s weighted-eCDF definition (multiplying values weights changes distribution’s support, weighting). package uses paper’s definition via cobalt::col_w_ks(). binomial() family binary responses. reference code fit binary models inside SuperLearner gaussian(link = "logit"). package uses binomial() throughout treatment models (binary-outcome prognostic models). train/test-inconsistent scale(). reference code applied scale() separately fitting prediction sets, standardizing different means SDs. package passes raw covariates engines (pinned engines either scale-invariant fit predict identical rows). Strict complete-case handling. reference Fasmd applied na.omit() covariate vector indexing full-length treatment weight vectors, silently misaligning rows presence missing data. psave() refuses incomplete data outright: NA treatment, covariates, outcome (used) error, never silent drop. sixth, minor note: candidate scores clipped averaging never re-clipped afterward (see Step 1); reference code’s clipping constants 0.01/0.99 exposed clip argument.

title: Getting Started with psAve
text: Propensity score (PS) analyses good propensity score model, practice analyst rarely knows model right. Logistic regression may miss nonlinearities interactions; flexible machine learning methods (classification trees, random forests, gradient boosting) capture can produce extreme scores unstable weights. Different candidate models often yield propensity scores “look reasonable” yet lead materially different effect estimates — form model dependence undermines credibility analysis. Rather committing single model, psAve constructs model-averaged propensity score: convex combination e‾(X)=∑mλmêm(X)\bar e(X) = \sum_m \lambda_m \hat e_m(X) candidate propensity scores, mixing weights λ\lambda chosen simplex grid optimize balance criterion, implementing method Kabata, Stuart & Shintani (2024). distinguishing feature method mixing weights asked balance. Covariate balance criteria treat covariates equally important, bias treatment effect matters balance covariates relate outcome. prognostic score — predicted outcome untreated condition, g(0,X)g(0, X), estimated untreated units (Hansen 2008) — summarizes exactly relationship, prognostic-score balance shown useful diagnostic propensity score methods (Stuart, Lee & Leacy 2013). psAve takes one step uses weighted standardized mean difference (model-averaged) prognostic score selection criterion propensity score mixing weights. simulations Kabata, Stuart & Shintani (2024), “Prog (Ave)” strategy gave lowest robust bias RMSE across 16 scenarios, compared single-model propensity scores model averaging targeted prediction accuracy covariate balance. result psave() deliberately modest: numeric vector propensity scores, designed handed MatchIt::matchit() distance measure WeightIt::weightit() propensity score, balance assessment via cobalt.

what: Doesn’t using the outcome bias my analysis?
title: Getting Started with psAve
text: natural first objection, design method answers . Two facts matter: prognostic model never sees treated outcomes. Following Hansen (2008), prognostic score g(0,X)g(0, X) estimated untreated units predicted everyone. γ\gamma mixing weights likewise selected using prediction error among untreated units . model pipeline uses treated-arm outcomes. selection criterion never contrasts outcomes arms. λ\lambda criterion balance statistic — weighted standardized mean difference prognostic score treatment groups. measures whether two groups look comparable outcome-relevant summary covariates; measure, driven toward, particular treatment effect estimate. point treated-versus-untreated outcome comparison computed design. preserves separation design stage analysis stage (Rubin 2001; Hansen 2008): everything psave() function covariates, treatment indicator, untreated units’ outcomes, exactly like prognostic-score balance diagnostics advocated Stuart, Lee & Leacy (2013). reasoning MatchIt’s documentation welcomes externally estimated distance measures long post-treatment information treated-untreated outcome contrast enters design. fuller discussion, exact formulas, vignette("method-details", package = "psAve").

title: Reproducing the published IPW workflow
text: published example estimates ATT fitting weighted generalized linear model outcome treatment within survey design, provides robust (sandwich) standard errors: coefficient treat IPW-ATT estimate effect treatment re78 among treated. Two honest caveats, consistent paper. what: psAve: Model-Averaged Propensity Scores Selected by Prognostic-Score Balance
title: psAve: Model-Averaged Propensity Scores Selected by Prognostic-Score Balance
text: Model-averaged propensity scores selected prognostic-score balance. psAve implements propensity score model averaging method Kabata, Stuart & Shintani (2024, BMC Medical Research Methodology). Instead committing single propensity score model, psave() fits several candidate models (logistic regression, CART, random forest, gradient boosting, SuperLearner wrapper), combines convex mixture whose weights chosen simplex grid optimize balance prognostic score — predicted outcome untreated condition, estimated untreated units (Hansen 2008). prognostic score summarizes covariates relate outcome, balancing targets exactly covariate directions drive bias treatment effect; paper’s simulations “Prog (Ave)” strategy achieved lowest robust bias RMSE across 16 scenarios. Covariate-balance (SMD, KS) prediction-accuracy (log loss) criteria paper also available. psAve companion MatchIt/WeightIt/cobalt ecosystem, replacement part . deliverable plain numeric vector propensity scores, handed MatchIt::matchit() distance measure WeightIt::weightit() propensity score (thin psave_match() / psave_weight() wrappers without retyping formula); balance assessment — including prognostic-score balance — works box via cobalt::bal.tab(); effect estimation stays belongs, MatchIt/WeightIt/survey/marginaleffects. Estimands: ATT (default) ATE.

what: Method details: relation to the paper's reference implementation — psave-details
title: Method details: relation to the paper's reference implementation — psave-details
text: psAve implements published method Kabata, Stuart Shintani (2024) rather reproducing reference code line line. Five documented defects/quirks reference implementation deliberately fixed (fix follows paper's stated definitions): Integer simplex grid. reference code enumerated mixing-weight grid expand.grid() kept rows passing exact floating-point rowSums(gr) == 1 test, silently dropping 10.6% valid grid points M = 4 candidates step 0.05 (1,584 1,771 kept). simplex_grid() enumerates integer compositions, every valid point present construction, enumeration order makes first-minimum tie-break reproducible rule. Proper weighted-eCDF KS statistic. reference Fks computed ks.test() covariate values multiplied weights, paper's weighted-eCDF definition. psAve computes \(\sup_x |F^w_1(x) - F^w_0(x)|\) weighted empirical CDFs arm (cobalt::col_w_ks() ). Proper binomial family. reference code fit binary-response SuperLearner models gaussian(link = "logit"); psAve uses binomial() throughout treatment models. per-set scale(). reference code standardized fitting prediction sets separately, inconsistent transformation. psAve passes raw covariates engines. Strict complete-case alignment. reference Fasmd applied na.omit() covariate using full-length treatment weight vectors, silently misaligning rows presence missing data. psAve refuses missing values used variable (error, never drop), names returned score vectors rownames(data).

KS criterion two conventions coincide binary columns.

title: Model-averaged propensity scores selected by prognostic-score balance — psave
text: formula two-sided formula treat ~ x1 + x2 + ..., exactly MatchIt::matchit(). right-hand side defines candidate-PS covariates balance covariates used smd/ks criteria. data data frame containing variables formula (outcome). Complete cases used variables REQUIRED; missing value error, never silent row drop. outcome outcome specification prognostic score: one-sided formula ~ y (formula right-hand side reused prognostic predictors) two-sided formula y ~ z1 + z2 (distinct prognostic specification). title: Model-averaged propensity scores selected by prognostic-score balance — psave
text: Candidate propensity scores. learner ps.methods fit \(n\) units predicts \(\hat e_m(X_i) = P(A_i = 1 \mid X_i)\) -sample; column clipped clip averaging. Extra user-supplied candidates given via ps.append validated, clipped identically, appended base candidates (ps.methods ps.matrix), first-minimum tie-break favors base set. Model-averaged prognostic score. learner prog.methods fit untreated units predicts \(\hat g_k(0, X_i)\) \(n\) units; prog.append columns appended candidates. mixing weights \(\gamma\) minimize unweighted untreated-set mean squared error $$\mathrm{MSE}(\gamma) = \mathrm{mean}_{:A_i=0}\bigl(Y_i - \textstyle\sum_k \gamma_k \hat g_k(0, X_i)\bigr)^2$$ simplex_grid(K, step); \(\bar g = \sum_k \gamma_k \hat g_k\). Model-averaged propensity score. mixing weights \(\lambda\) minimize criterion simplex_grid(M, step), grid row implies \(\bar e_\lambda = \sum_m \lambda_m \hat e_m\) weights \(W_i\) .

title: Model-averaged propensity scores selected by prognostic-score balance — psave
text: \(\bar e_i\) averaged propensity score: $$\mathrm{ATT:}\quad W_i = 1 \; (A_i = 1), \qquad W_i = \bar e_i / (1 - \bar e_i) \; (A_i = 0);$$ $$\mathrm{ATE:}\quad W_i = 1 / \bar e_i \; (A_i = 1), \qquad W_i = 1 / (1 - \bar e_i) \; (A_i = 0).$$ identical WeightIt::get_w_from_ps() estimand.

text: grid row \(\lambda\): logloss \(-\mathrm{mean}\{A_i \log \bar e_i + (1 - A_i) \log(1 - \bar e_i)\}\) (finite clipping). smd mean covariate columns \(j\) $$\mathrm{ASMD}_j = \Bigl| \frac{\sum_{A_i=1} W_i X_{ij}}{\sum_{A_i=1} W_i} - \frac{\sum_{A_i=0} W_i X_{ij}}{\sum_{A_i=0} W_i} \Bigr| / s_j,$$ \(s_j\) unweighted sample SD \(X_j\) s.d.denom group (treated group estimands, per paper's supplement). ks mean covariates proper weighted-eCDF Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistic \(\sup_x |F^w_1(x) - F^w_0(x)|\) \(F^w_a(x) = \sum_{:A_i=} W_i 1(X_{ij} \le x) / \sum_{:A_i=} W_i\); binary columns equals absolute difference weighted proportions. prog \(\mathrm{ASMD}\) formula applied single column \(\bar g\) (prog.target = "average") \(\hat g_k\) (prog.target names learner). DEFAULT. prog.target names single prognostic candidate, criterion.value weighted ASMD candidate's prognostic score, whereas prog column diagnostics table always refers model-averaged prognostic score \(\bar g\); two values therefore need coincide, summary.psave() prints footnote effect. Faithful published method, smd prog criteria standardize columns – including binary ones – plain unweighted sample SD (uniform sample-SD standardization; bin.vars = FALSE passed cobalt::col_w_smd() every column). display-oriented balance component bal.tab.psave() use cobalt's native conventions instead; see vignette("method-details", "psAve").

text: Enumerates points probability simplex \(\{\lambda \[0,1]^M : \sum_m \lambda_m = 1\}\) regular grid increment step, using integer arithmetic: \(S = 1/\code{step}\) steps, every integer composition \((c_1, \dots, c_M)\) \(\sum_m c_m = S\) \(c_m \ge 0\) listed returned \(c / S\). grid psave() searches propensity score mixing weights \(\lambda\) prognostic mixing weights \(\gamma\).

title: Enumerate the discretized probability simplex — simplex_grid
text: M Integer; number mixture components (grid columns). step Numeric; grid increment. Must evenly divide 1 (checked integer arithmetic: n_steps = round(1/step), call errors unless abs(n_steps * step - 1) < 1e-8). Default 0.05, value used Kabata, Stuart Shintani (2024).

title: Enumerate the discretized probability simplex — simplex_grid
text: numeric matrix \(\binom{S + M - 1}{M - 1}\) rows M columns; row sums 1 exactly (integer arithmetic single final division \(S\)).

title: Enumerate the discretized probability simplex — simplex_grid
text: number grid points exactly \(\binom{S + M - 1}{M - 1}\); e.g., M = 4, step = 0.05 gives choose(23, 3) = 1771 points. grid built integer compositions, every valid point present construction; reference implementation paper instead filtered expand.grid() rows floating-point rowSums(gr) == 1 test, silently dropped 10.6% valid points M = 4, step = 0.05. Enumeration order (tie-breaking rule). Rows generated recursive descent: \(c_1\) runs \(S\) 0; within value \(c_1\), \(c_2\) runs remainder 0; . first row therefore \((1, 0, \dots, 0)\) last row \((0, \dots, 0, 1)\). grid searches psave() resolve ties taking first row attaining minimum, within 1e-9 relative tolerance minimum, ties favor learners listed earlier ps.methods / prog.methods. tolerance deliberate: criterion values computed floating-point matrix algebra whose lowest-order bits can differ across BLAS implementations, exact bitwise .min() reproducible across machines, whereas tolerant first-minimum rule .

title: psAve 1.0.0
text: Initial release, implementing Kabata, Stuart & Shintani (2024), BMC Medical Research Methodology 24:228, doi:10.1186/s12874-024-02350-y. psave(): model-averaged propensity scores convex combination candidate models ("glm", "rpart", "ranger", "xgboost" default; "SL.*" SuperLearner wrapper; user-supplied ps.matrix/prog.matrix), mixing weights selected simplex grid. ps.append / prog.append: extra user-supplied candidate score columns (vector length n, matrix/-numeric data frame unique column names) appended candidates ps.methods/ps.matrix prog.methods/prog.matrix. Appended propensity columns validated (strictly (0, 1)) clipped like every candidate; grid tie-breaking favors base candidates. Supplying prog.matrix prog.append without outcome explicit error (prognostic candidates require outcome; gamma selected outcome-prediction MSE among untreated units). Four selection criteria paper: "prog" (weighted ASMD model-averaged prognostic score, recommended default; per-candidate targets via prog.target), "smd", "ks", "logloss". Estimands: ATT (default) ATE, supplement’s estimand-specific weight formulas. Prognostic scores per Hansen (2008): candidate outcome models fit untreated units ; mixing weights gamma selected unweighted untreated-set MSE. gaussian() binomial() outcome families. average = FALSE vertex mode selects single best candidate propensity score chosen criterion. Ecosystem integration: fit$ps drops MatchIt::matchit(distance = ) WeightIt::weightit(ps = ); psave_match() / psave_weight() wrappers reuse stored formula data eliminate row-misalignment; cobalt::bal.tab() works directly psave objects. Full S3 suite: print() (literal next call), summary() (mixing weights, -criteria diagnostics table, full balance table), plot() ("balance", "distribution", "criterion"), fitted(), weights(), predict() (keep.fits = TRUE). Exported utilities: simplex_grid() (integer-composition simplex enumeration defining tie-breaking order) psave_criteria() (four criteria propensity score vector). Documented fixes relative paper’s reference code: integer-arithmetic simplex grid (reference’s floating-point rowSums == 1 filter silently dropped ~10.6% grid points); proper weighted-eCDF KS statistic; binomial() family binary responses; train/test-inconsistent scale(); strict complete-case handling (NAs error, never dropped). See vignette("method-details", package = "psAve"). Three vignettes: Getting Started (matching workflow), Reproducing published IPW workflow (ATT/ATE weighting survey::svyglm()), Method details.
